questions.clue: Newspapers used this euphemistic & alliterative 2-word term to describe a 1923 attack by a white mob on a Black Florida town
In 1823 this eponymous man was elevated to superintendent of the Survey of India
In 1523 the Spanish fought a bloody battle in this C. American country & soon founded the city of Granada on a lake there
For signing a treaty with the rebellious Scots, in 1323 the earl of Carlisle met with this grisly 3-part (or un-part) punishment
Shakespeare left these 2 men a little something in his will; they repaid him by collecting his plays in the 1623 First Folio
You don't have to be a follower of Antisthenes to be one of these doubting downers

[IMAGE CLUE]
Woo-hoo! It's the fancier name for this colorful display
Derived from Greek, it's an 11-letter word for a werewolf

[IMAGE CLUE]
It's the growing of plants without soil but in other mediums & with nutrient-infused water
This ancient city of Phoenicia was a trading center for papyrus & was also the cradle of the Phoenician alphabet
Vermicomposting uses these annelids, particularly red wigglers, to aid in organic farming
Putting "wheel" in front of this word makes it a helpful farm item; otherwise, it's just a term for a castrated pig
Britain's Royal Horticultural Soc. says this strategy means growing "on a different part of the vegetable plot each year"
This branch of agriculture is the science of raising farmyard animals; sounds like matrimony is involved

[IMAGE CLUE]
Created by removing bad traits from rapeseed, such as its name, this plant full of useful oil has been grown in the U.S. since 1988
Instructions to this Hasbro game make things pretty clear... "Your goal is simple: to take over the world"
Hoops coach Geno Auriemma has led this university to 6 undefeated seasons; one was 40-0, & the 2nd of 4 straight national titles
He eked out a win in a 2007 presidential referendum in Syria, taking 97.6% of the vote, raising the question... who dared vote against him?
You can trust us, by 1880 this company controlled the refining of at least 90% of U.S.-produced oil
Though common, this totally epoch term for the time when human activity came to dominate Earth was denied official adoption in 2024
An ideal society in the Pacific attracts the envy of the world in this author's "Island", a counterpoint to "Brave New World"
Sir Francis Bacon described a utopian domain based on science in "The New" this mythic landmass
One of the most important works by Plato, it outlines the politics & ethics of his vision of a perfect nation
What "utopia" means, it completes the title of William Morris' book about a place without politics or poverty, "News from..."
In the 10th century al-Farabi's "The Virtuous City" posited such a place in this locale, Muhammad's last home

categories.name: THE WORLD REVOLVES AROUND JASON KELCE

questions.clue:
[IMAGE CLUE]
(Jason Kelce delivers the clue.) In 2023, along with yours truly, Pedro Pascal & Usher were finalists for this honor from People magazine; Patrick Dempsey won, but this ain't over, McDreamy

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Jason Kelce delivers the clue.) I was a key part of our quarterback sneak known as the "Tush Push", so I always smile knowingly when I hear their song, "Ah, push it, push good / Ah, push it, push it real good"

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Jason Kelce delivers the clue.) "Here we stand before your door / As we stood the year before / Give us whiskey, give us gin / Open the door and let us in" is a poem from this group, a Philly tradition; I dressed as one at our Super Bowl victory parade

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Jason Kelce delivers the clue.) I probably had a different experience at the University of Cincinnati than this man who was Lincoln's treasury secretary & the face on the $10,000 bill

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Jason Kelce delivers the clue.) It's great being a girl dad, but not for a medieval king; Philip V of France & his wife Joan of this wine-producing region had only surviving daughters & no heir to the throne

final.clue: This man was the 1st to be governor of one state & then senator from another; 173 years later, Mitt Romney became the second
